# Quote Lifecycle in Postgres — Design (v1, ops channel)

**Date:** 2026-06-30
**Status:** approved for planning
**Milestone:** M11 (follows the quote engine PR #2 and the internal quoting tool PR #3)

## Goal

Make Postgres the single source of truth for **every price we hand out**, so we can
answer "are we losing leads because we're too expensive?" — starting with the
ops/WhatsApp channel that the internal quoting tool serves.

## Scope

**In (v1):** persistence + lifecycle for quotes generated by the internal quoting tool
(`/admin/quote`). Save a priced quote, list quotes, move them through a status flow,
record why a lost quote was lost.

**Out (deferred, by decision):**
- **Web-channel capture.** The frozen front-end prices client-side and only calls the
  backend at booking-create. Capturing abandoned web quotes needs a labelled front-end
  change; we wire the front-end in *after* the pricing logic is proven through the tool.
- Lost-reason enum + an analytics/conversion dashboard (v1 stores the raw data).
- Quote versioning / re-quote threading.
- Auto-linking a won quote to the booking it became (column exists; population is later).

## Decisions (recorded)

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| D1 | **Channel scope = ops only** for v1; web deferred | Prove pricing via the tool first (user call) |
| D2 | **Storage = JSONB snapshot + flat columns** (Approach A) | Three product shapes differ structurally; JSONB is replayable + avoids premature normalization; matches existing `tripRequests` "normalise later" pattern |
| D3 | **Status flow = `draft → sent → won \| lost \| expired`** | Covers the real lead flow with room to grow; transitions are manual in the tool |
| D4 | **`lost_reason` = nullable free-text**, optional | Directly serves the "too expensive?" goal; costs nothing; not forced |
| D5 | **`/estimate` stays stateless**; a separate **`/save`** persists | Saving is an explicit click, not every keystroke |
| D6 | **Re-price = a new row** (no versioning in v1) | YAGNI; group by contact later if needed |
| D7 | **Fold admin-key auth** into this work — gate every XHR/data route (`/places`, `/distance`, `/estimate`, `/save`, `/list`, `/:id`, `/patch`) with `x-admin-key`; leave only `GET /` (the JS shell) open | We now persist customer PII, and `/estimate` already leaks margin/cost; `GET /` can't carry a header on a browser navigation, and the shell is useless without a valid key on its fetches |
| D8 | Re-price server-side inside `/save` before persisting | Never trust a client-supplied total — server is authoritative |

## Data model — `quotes` table (migration 0009)

| column | type | notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | uuid pk | `defaultRandom()` |
| `reference` | text unique | short human code, e.g. `Q-7F3K`, for pasting into WhatsApp |
| `channel` | text notNull default `'ops'` | future-proofs for `'web'` |
| `status` | text notNull default `'draft'` | `draft` \| `sent` \| `won` \| `lost` \| `expired` |
| `lost_reason` | text nullable | optional; free-text for now |
| `product` | text notNull | `private` \| `chauffeur` (`shared` later) |
| `vehicle` | text nullable | `car` \| `van` (null for shared) |
| `customer_name` | text nullable | lightweight lead — not a heavy `customers` row |
| `customer_contact` | text nullable | WhatsApp number or email |
| `total_cents` | integer notNull | flat, for analytics |
| `currency` | text notNull | ISO code (`USD`) |
| `rate_card_version` | text notNull | which rate card priced it (freeze context) |
| `margin_cents` | integer nullable | engine margin estimate at quote time |
| `request_json` | jsonb notNull | the engine `QuoteRequest` verbatim |
| `result_json` | jsonb notNull | the engine `QuoteResult` verbatim (line items, deposit, …) |
| `converted_booking_id` | uuid nullable → bookings.id | set when a won quote becomes a booking (population deferred) |
| `notes` | text nullable | free notes |
| `created_at` | timestamptz notNull default now | |
| `updated_at` | timestamptz notNull default now | bumped on every mutation |
| `sent_at` | timestamptz nullable | set on `→ sent` |
| `decided_at` | timestamptz nullable | set on `→ won/lost/expired` |

Notes:
- `request_json` / `result_json` store the exact engine I/O so a quote is replayable and
  the quoted price is frozen even if the rate card changes.
- `reference` is generated server-side (short, unambiguous alphabet).
- No FK to `customers` — ops leads are lightweight; a customer row is only created if they
  actually book. `converted_booking_id` is the eventual bridge.

## API — extends `internalQuoteRoutes` (`/admin/quote`)

**Auth (D7):** every XHR/data route is gated by `x-admin-key` (the existing admin-key
pattern) — that means `/places`, `/distance`, `/estimate` (which already exposes margin/cost)
and the new `/save`, `/list`, `/:id`, `/patch`. Only `GET /` (the JS shell) stays open, since
a browser navigation can't send a header; the shell prompts for the key once, stores it in
`localStorage`, and attaches it to every fetch. Missing/wrong key on a gated route → 401.

| method + path | purpose |
|---|---|
| `POST /admin/quote/save` | Persist the current quote. Body = the same `ToolRequest` shape as `/estimate`, plus optional `name`/`contact`/`notes`. **Re-prices server-side** (D8), builds the row, returns `{ id, reference, status: 'draft' }`. |
| `GET /admin/quote/list` | Recent quotes, newest first. Query filters: `status`, `product`, `from`/`to` date. Returns flat summary rows (id, reference, status, product, customer, total, created_at). |
| `GET /admin/quote/:id` | Full quote incl. `request_json`/`result_json` (for re-opening in the tool). |
| `PATCH /admin/quote/:id` | Update `status` (+ auto-stamp `sent_at`/`decided_at`), `lost_reason`, `notes`. Validates status transitions loosely (any → any allowed for v1; timestamps stamped on entry to sent/decided). |

`/save` and `/estimate` share the same pricing path (the existing `toEngineRequest` +
`quote()`), so a saved quote's total always equals what the tool previewed.

## Repo pattern (mirrors the codebase exactly)

- `QuoteRepo` interface in `api/src/db/quoteRepo.ts` with `InMemoryQuoteRepo` (tests/dev).
- `PostgresQuoteRepo` in `api/src/db/postgresQuoteRepo.ts`.
- `quotes` Drizzle table added to `api/src/db/schema.ts`.
- SQL migration `api/src/db/migrations/0009_quotes.sql` (+ drizzle meta) and mirrored in
  `api/drizzle/`.
- Wired through `AppDeps` (`quotes?: QuoteRepo`) and injected into `internalQuoteRoutes`,
  exactly like `bookings`, `payments`, `rideOps`, etc.

## Tool UI (minimal additions to `quote-tool.html`)

- A **Save quote** button on the Pricing Summary (captures the priced result + optional
  customer name/contact/notes → `POST /save`, shows the returned reference).
- A **Recent quotes** panel (calls `/list`): reference, customer, total, status pill; each
  row has a status control (`PATCH`) to mark sent/won/lost(+reason)/expired.

On load the shell prompts once for the admin key (stored in `localStorage`) and attaches it
as `x-admin-key` to every fetch (D7). The UI stays a thin client over the server-authoritative
endpoints.

## Error handling

- `/save`: re-prices via the engine; if pricing throws (e.g. unknown extra, no legs), return
  422 with the engine message — identical to `/estimate`. Distance auto-resolution reuses
  the `/estimate` path (400 when a leg has no km and no resolvable distance).
- `PATCH`: unknown id → 404; invalid status value → 400.
- Admin-key missing/wrong on a gated route → 401.

## Testing

Follows the "prove red → green" contract. Coverage:
- **`quoteRepo` (InMemory):** save assigns id + reference + timestamps; list ordering +
  filters; get-by-id; patch stamps `sent_at`/`decided_at` and updates `lost_reason`/notes;
  unknown-id behavior.
- **Route integration (`internalQuote.test.ts` additions):** `/save` persists and returns a
  reference; the saved `total_cents` equals `/estimate`'s total for the same input; `/save`
  re-prices server-side (a bogus client total is ignored); `/list` filters by status/product;
  `/patch` transitions + stamps; auth — gated routes 401 without the key, 200 with it;
  `/save` 422 on an unpriceable trip.
- **Postgres repo:** covered by the existing `postgres.test.ts` harness pattern (skipped
  without a DB, run in CI with one), asserting round-trip of a JSONB quote.
- **Browser e2e (Playwright):** price → Save → assert a reference appears → mark status →
  assert it reflects in the Recent panel.

## Migration & rollout

- Additive migration only (new table); no change to existing tables, so no risk to the live
  booking flow.
- `converted_booking_id` FK is nullable and unpopulated in v1 — safe to add now, wire later.

## Open items carried forward

- Web-channel quote capture (needs the labelled front-end change) — the next phase once the
  pricing logic is validated through the tool.
- Lost-reason enum + conversion dashboard.
- Auto-link won quote → booking (populate `converted_booking_id`).
- Prod MUST set ADMIN_API_KEY (auth enforced only when configured) — added to the go-live checklist.
